title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, and Status Contracts · Go Build Room
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first resource, method, status, and retry semantics table for http semantics, resources, methods, and status contracts. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of resource, method, status, and retry semantics table while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method semantics independently and preserve them through handler behavior.
Choose status, Location, validators, and response content for create, replace, partial update, asynchronous work, and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Representation State
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 8047 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, status, and durable effect; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test method semantics against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, and changed resource state.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Semantics Changed Request Proof, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Semantics Changed Request Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.
